sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Donegal Mutual buys $305,880 in Donegal Group stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Donegal Mutual Insurance CO, a significant owner of Donegal Group Inc. (NASDAQ:DGICA), recently acquired 16,000 shares of Class A Common Stock for a total of $305,880. These transactions increased Donegal Mutual's direct ownership to over 13.6 million shares of Class A and 4.7 million shares of Class B Common Stock. The purchases occurred shortly after Donegal Group reported Q3 2025 earnings that surpassed analyst expectations, despite a revenue shortfall.

feed.title: New to The Street Broadcasts Show #716 Tonight at 6:30 PM ET on Bloomberg Television Featuring Stardust Power (NASDAQ:SDST), BioVie (NASDAQ:BIVI), Roadzen (NASDAQ:RDZN), with Special Segments from Kelsey Grammer on KAREN and Andrew
feed.summary: New to The Street will broadcast Show #716 tonight at 6:30 PM ET on Bloomberg Television, featuring companies Stardust Power (NASDAQ:SDST), BioVie (NASDAQ:BIVI), and Roadzen (NASDAQ:RDZN). The episode will also include special segments with Kelsey Grammer discussing his book "KAREN" and Andrew Shaw from 250DAYS.ORG. This broadcast is sponsored programming supported by national commercials from several companies, highlighting New to The Street's "Predictable Media" model.

feed.title: Bitfarms Faces Downgrade, Eyes on Liquidity and Legislation Timing
feed.summary: Bitfarms (BITF) has been downgraded by Keefe Bruyette to Market Perform due to concerns over liquidity risks, increased leverage, and high capital expenditure. The company's stock is down amidst uncertainties in cryptocurrency market regulation and delays in crypto-friendly legislation. Key financial metrics show negative gross margins and return on equity, highlighting operational inefficiencies and the need for strategic shifts and cost streamlining.

feed.title: BioNTech Adds Chief People Officer to Management Board to Support Oncology Growth Strategy
feed.summary: BioNTech SE has appointed Kylie Jimenez as its new Chief People Officer, effective March 1, 2026, to lead the company's global people strategy. This move supports BioNTech's goal of becoming a multi-product oncology company by 2030, emphasizing human capital for long-term growth. Analysts currently rate BNTX as a Hold with a $121.00 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst assesses it as Neutral due to challenging financial performance balanced by a strong balance sheet and strategic oncology advancements.

feed.title: Share buyback and dividend set at Canadian Pacific (CP)
feed.summary: Canadian Pacific Kansas City Limited (CP) has renewed its normal course issuer bid, allowing for the repurchase and cancellation of up to 44,865,624 Common Shares, representing about 5% of its outstanding shares, running from February 2, 2026, to February 1, 2027. The company's Board also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.228 per share, payable on April 27, 2026, to shareholders of record on March 27, 2026. Additionally, Marc Parent was appointed to the Board of Directors effective January 27, 2026.

feed.title: Toyota smashes global sales records, again
feed.summary: Toyota Motor Corporation achieved its sixth consecutive year as the world's top-selling vehicle manufacturer in 2025, setting a new sales record with 11,322,575 vehicles delivered globally. This figure includes sales from Toyota, Lexus, Daihatsu, and Hino, widening the gap with the second-placed Volkswagen Group. Electrified vehicles represented a significant portion of Toyota and Lexus sales, with hybrids being the most popular, and battery-electric vehicle deliveries more than quadrupling.

feed.title: Wintrust Financial EVP Hahnfeld sells $45,665 in stock
feed.summary: Jeffrey D. Hahnfeld, EVP at Wintrust Financial Corp, sold 314 shares of common stock for $45,665 on January 27, 2026. This transaction comes amidst positive developments for the company, including a dividend increase and optimistic price target adjustments from several analyst firms, reflecting strong growth prospects and operational performance.

feed.title: CARPENTER TECHNOLOGY CORP SEC 10-Q Report
feed.summary: Carpenter Technology Corp (CRS) released its 10-Q report for Q2 fiscal year 2025, detailing significant financial growth with net sales up 8% to $728.0 million and operating income increasing 31% to $155.2 million. The company reported strong performance in both its Specialty Alloys Operations (SAO) and Performance Engineered Products (PEP) segments, driven by realized price increases, improved product mix, and operational efficiencies. Carpenter Technology anticipates continued growth, especially in the Aerospace and Defense and Energy markets, supported by new production launches and expanding margins.

feed.title: Donaldson Company taps COO Richard Lewis as next CEO
feed.summary: Donaldson Company, a global leader in filtration products, has announced that Chief Operating Officer Richard Lewis will succeed Tod Carpenter as President and CEO, effective March 2, 2026. Carpenter, who has served the company for 30 years, including 11 as CEO, will transition to Executive Chairman. The board expressed confidence in Lewis's deep understanding of the business and ability to continue the company's growth trajectory.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines shares soar to highest levels in years
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines reported record revenue for both the fourth quarter and the full year, following a period of significant changes including layoffs, new products, and ending its long-standing open-seating policy. This strong financial performance has led to a surge in its share price. The airline's shares have reached their highest levels in years on the back of a big forecast for 2026.

feed.title: Nordson schedules Q1 2026 earnings release and investor webcast in February
feed.summary: Nordson Corporation announced it will release its first quarter fiscal year 2026 earnings on February 18, 2026, after market close. The company will host a quarterly investor webcast on February 19, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Nordson is a precision technology company serving various end markets globally.

feed.title: Winter Storm Fern Tests Southern’s Grid Resilience And Investor Thesis
feed.summary: Southern Company's subsidiary, Georgia Power, successfully restored power to over 214,000 customers after Winter Storm Fern, demonstrating strong grid resilience and operational execution. This event provides investors with insight into the company's ability to maintain critical services during severe weather, reinforcing the narrative for long-term reliability and the importance of ongoing capital spending on infrastructure. While reliable storm response supports regulatory confidence and dividend stability, analysts note that interest payments are not well covered by earnings, suggesting a need to monitor future borrowing for infrastructure hardening.

feed.title: Should Lennox’s Record 2025 Margins Offset a Weak Quarter for Lennox International (LII) Investors?
feed.summary: Lennox International reported full-year 2025 results with sales of US$5,195.3 million and net income of US$805.8 million, despite a weak fourth quarter with an 11.2% revenue decline and missed operating margins. However, the company achieved record annual margins above 20% and forecasts 6-7% revenue growth for 2026, driven by acquisitions and cost-cutting measures. Investors are weighing these record annual margins against the weaker quarterly performance and the importance of execution on cost savings and acquisitions for future growth.

feed.title: Stryker’s 2025 Q4 earnings exceed Wall Street predictions
feed.summary: Stryker reported strong fourth-quarter 2025 results, with adjusted EPS of $4.47 and revenue of $7.2 billion, both surpassing Wall Street expectations. The company achieved full-year adjusted EPS of $13.63, an 11.8% increase from 2024, driven by sales growth and improved operating margins. Stryker anticipates continued organic net sales growth and increased adjusted earnings per share for 2026, fueled by innovation and new product introductions.

feed.title: A Look At Eversource Energy’s Valuation As It Lifts Dividend And Refocuses On Regulated Utilities
feed.summary: Eversource Energy (ES) has increased its quarterly dividend to $0.7875 per share and is shifting its focus away from offshore wind to regulated utility operations. Despite a recent share price decline over 90 days, the company shows a positive one-year return. Analysts view Eversource as 6% undervalued, with a fair value target of $73.33, citing positive regulatory developments and expected long-term stability in earnings and cash flow.

feed.title: Weyerhaeuser swings to an adjusted loss in fourth quarter on weak wood product prices
feed.summary: Weyerhaeuser (WY) reported an adjusted loss in its fourth quarter due to weak wood product prices. The news was published by Reuters through TradingView.

feed.title: Spinal Cord Injury Market to Evolve Rapidly Over the Next Decade by 2034, DelveInsight Observes | Kringle Pharma, Neuroplast, AbbVie, and Mitsubishi Tanabe Pharma America
feed.summary: The Spinal Cord Injury (SCI) market is predicted to grow significantly by 2034, driven by increasing prevalence, awareness, and the launch of new pipeline products from companies like Kringle Pharma, Neuroplast, AbbVie, and Mitsubishi Tanabe Pharma America. DelveInsight's report provides an in-depth analysis of market trends, epidemiology, emerging therapies, and market dynamics across the United States, EU4, UK, and Japan. Key drivers include rising SCI incidence and clinical trial activity, while barriers include limited treatment options and high costs.

feed.title: Aptiv PLC (APTV) Increases Despite Market Slip: Here's What You Need to Know
feed.summary: Aptiv PLC (APTV) saw a 1.31% increase in its stock price, closing at $78.83, despite a general market slip, outperforming the S&P 500. The company is set to release its Q4 2025 earnings report on February 2, 2026, with analysts projecting a 4.00% EPS increase and 3.5% revenue growth. APTV currently holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold) and shows an attractive valuation with a Forward P/E of 9.51 and a PEG ratio of 0.95.

feed.title: Eastman Chemical (EMN) Q4 Earnings and Revenues Miss Estimates
feed.summary: Eastman Chemical (EMN) reported Q4 earnings of $0.75 per share and revenues of $1.97 billion, missing Zacks Consensus Estimates. The specialty chemicals maker has struggled to meet revenue estimates in the last four quarters, leading to a Zacks Rank #4 (Sell) for its stock. Investors are advised to consider the company's earnings outlook and industry trends, as well as upcoming reports from competitors like DuPont de Nemours, when evaluating EMN's future performance.

feed.title: Emerson Electric (EMR) Gains As Market Dips: What You Should Know
feed.summary: Emerson Electric (EMR) saw a positive movement of +1.55% in its latest trading session despite a general market dip. The company is anticipated to report earnings of $1.41 per share, reflecting a 2.17% year-over-year growth, on February 3, 2026, with revenue projected to be $4.35 billion. Emerson Electric currently holds a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold) and trades at a Forward P/E ratio of 22.9, which is a discount compared to its industry's average.

feed.title: Sanghi Steve, Microchip Technology CEO, sells $7.9 million in stock
feed.summary: Microchip Technology CEO, Steve Sanghi, sold 98,814 shares of common stock valued at approximately $7.9 million on January 28, 2026. This transaction was conducted under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. The article also mentions recent positive developments for both Microchip Technology and Texas Instruments, including analyst upgrades and new product introductions.

feed.title: (DCBO) Strategic Market Analysis (DCBO:CA)
feed.summary: This article provides a strategic market analysis for Docebo Inc. (DCBO:CA), offering trading plans with specific buy and short targets, along with stop-loss recommendations. It also includes AI-generated signal ratings for near, mid, and long terms, and lists numerous recent articles related to DCBO:CA's performance and trading strategies.

feed.title: KLA Q2 2026 slides: 17% revenue growth, AI demand drives strong performance
feed.summary: KLA Corporation reported strong Q2 fiscal 2026 earnings, exceeding market expectations with $3.3 billion in revenue and non-GAAP EPS of $8.85. The company's performance was driven by AI demand across wafer fabrication equipment markets, particularly in advanced packaging and process control technologies, leading to a 3.53% stock price increase. KLA also highlighted its robust financial health, consistent free cash flow growth, and commitment to shareholder returns through dividends and share repurchases.

feed.title: Wells Fargo CEO Charlie Scharf gets 28% pay boost to $40 million
feed.summary: Wells Fargo CEO Charlie Scharf received a 28% pay increase for 2025, bringing his compensation to $40 million. The bank's board justified the raise by citing his leadership in addressing compliance issues, closing regulatory penalties, and contributing to rising earnings and revenue. This compensation places Scharf among other Wall Street leaders seeing higher payouts after a strong year.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Adds New Ocean Class and River Cruise Ships to Orderbook
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Group has announced a significant expansion to its cruise line offerings, including a new class of ocean cruise ships named "Discovery" and a doubling of its river cruise operations to 20 ships. The company also reported strong financial performance for 2025 and an optimistic outlook for 2026, leading to a nearly 19 percent surge in its stock price. These new orders contribute to an industry-wide orderbook valued at over $70 billion.

feed.title: Action 13 gets leaking utility pole removed from Richmond man's backyard
feed.summary: A Richmond homeowner successfully had a leaking utility pole removed from his backyard after Action 13 intervened on his behalf. The homeowner, Ivo Quinonez, reported that CenterPoint Energy had installed the pole without his permission, and it was spraying a dark substance onto his property. After Action 13 got involved, an environmental crew cleaned his backyard, and CenterPoint Energy committed to removing the utility pole.

feed.title: Caesars, MGM Earnings at Risk Following Las Vegas GGR Report
feed.summary: A disappointing December gross gaming revenue (GGR) report for the Las Vegas Strip, showing a 6% year-over-year decline, puts the upcoming earnings reports for Caesars Entertainment and MGM Resorts International at risk. An analyst noted the weak GGR highlights concerns for these operators due to their heavy dependence on the Las Vegas market, which accounts for significant portions of their revenue. In contrast, local gaming segments in Las Vegas show strength, potentially benefiting Boyd Gaming and Red Rock Resorts.

feed.title: Cisco Foundation AI debuts agentic security tools to protect autonomous AI systems
feed.summary: Cisco Foundation AI has launched a new suite of agentic security tools designed to help enterprises secure increasingly autonomous AI systems. These tools, which include a reasoning model for cybersecurity and an adaptive AI search framework, aim to address the challenges traditional security approaches face with modern AI workflows. They are focused on enabling AI systems to reason through complex security problems and execute workflows while maintaining transparency and human oversight.

feed.title: Campbell Soup Stock: Slow?Burn Comeback Or Classic Defensive Trap?
feed.summary: Campbell Soup Co. (CPB) has shown quiet market outperformance, driven by price hikes, a strong snacks portfolio, and a disciplined balance sheet. While still considered a defensive holding, analysts are becoming more constructive, noting its evolution into a broader consumer staples powerhouse. The article explores the company's strategic shift towards snacks and operational efficiency, making it an intriguing option for investors prioritizing stability and consistent returns over hyper-growth.

feed.title: Donaldson names COO Richard Lewis as new CEO effective March
feed.summary: Donaldson Company, Inc. (NYSE:DCI) has appointed its Chief Operating Officer, Richard Lewis, as the new President and CEO, effective March 2, 2026. Lewis will succeed Tod Carpenter, who will become executive chairman. The filtration company, with a market capitalization of $11.88 billion, has shown strong financial performance, including a 47% return over the past year and recent earnings exceeding expectations.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Daniel O'Day Sells 10,000 Shares of Gilead Sciences Inc (GILD)
feed.summary: Daniel O'Day, Chairman & CEO of Gilead Sciences Inc (GILD), sold 10,000 shares of the company on January 28, 2026, bringing his total ownership to 556,698 shares. This sale is part of a trend for O'Day, who has sold 205,640 shares over the past year with no purchases, and for the company which has seen 39 insider sells and 0 buys in the same period. Gilead Sciences Inc's stock is currently trading at $139.89, significantly overvalued compared to its GF Value of $89.43.

feed.title: Intuit to Announce Second-Quarter Fiscal Year 2026 Results on Feb. 26
feed.summary: Intuit Inc. (Nasdaq: INTU) is scheduled to announce its second-quarter fiscal year 2026 financial results on February 26, after the market closes. Company executives will host a conference call at 1:30 p.m. Pacific time to discuss the results, which will be accessible live on Intuit's investor relations website. A replay of the call will also be available for one week following the live event.

feed.title: Bain Capital Specialty Finance Signs Fourth Supplemental Indenture With U.S. Bank Trust Company
feed.summary: Bain Capital Specialty Finance (BCSF) has signed a Fourth Supplemental Indenture with U.S. Bank Trust Company to issue $350 million in 5.950% senior notes due 2031. The net proceeds, approximately $342.5 million, will be used to repay secured indebtedness and for general corporate purposes. These notes are unsecured and unsubordinated obligations of the company.

feed.title: Altimmune closes $75 million registered direct offering
feed.summary: Altimmune, Inc. has successfully closed a $75 million registered direct offering. The biopharmaceutical company plans to use the proceeds to fund its upcoming Phase 3 trial for pemvidutide, a drug candidate for MASH, and for general corporate purposes. Despite a strong financial position, analysts do not expect the company to be profitable this year.

feed.title: Parker-Hannifin (PH) Q2 2026 Earnings Transcript
feed.summary: Parker-Hannifin reported record Q2 2026 results with $5.2 billion in sales, 6.6% organic growth, and 27.1% adjusted segment operating margin. Adjusted EPS grew 17% to $7.65, driven by strong operational performance and share repurchases. The company increased its full-year organic sales growth guidance to 4-6% and adjusted EPS guidance to $30.70, while also announcing progress on the Filtration Group acquisition, expected to close within 6-12 months and deliver $220 million in cost synergies.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Upgrades Cintas (CTAS) and Names it a Top Pick for 2026
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has upgraded Cintas Corporation (CTAS) to Overweight and raised its price target to $245, naming it a top pick for 2026 in business and information services. The upgrade is based on solid underlying fundamentals and pricing power, despite valuation compression in 2025. Cintas is also pursuing a renewed takeover bid for UniFirst, offering $275 per share, aiming to consolidate the uniform rental market.

feed.title: Citi Turns More Positive on PPG as Housing Tailwinds Emerge
feed.summary: Citi raised its price target on PPG Industries, Inc. to $127 from $120, maintaining a Buy rating, due to an anticipated supportive housing environment in 2027 that could boost earnings in late 2026. PPG reported strong fourth-quarter 2025 results with $3.9 billion in net sales and 3% organic sales growth, driven by higher pricing and volumes across all regions. The company's Global Architectural Coatings segment showed particular improvement, with a sequential recovery in project-related demand in Mexico and solid retail sales.

feed.title: Research Alert: CFRA Reiterates Sell View On Shares Of Pultegroup, Inc.
feed.summary: CFRA has reiterated a "Sell" rating on shares of Pultegroup, Inc. The article also includes a brief mention of Unity Software's fourth-quarter financial results, noting a revenue increase of 35% year-over-year to $609 million and a quarterly loss of 66 cents per share.

feed.title: Why This Micron Rally Holds (NASDAQ:MU)
feed.summary: Micron Technology's rally is attributed to a structural shift in the memory market, driven by AI demand, which is causing extended supply shortages and reshaping pricing. High-bandwidth memory (HBM) demand is projected to grow over 25% annually, turning memory into a critical AI bottleneck and expanding towards a $60 billion market. Despite a 6.5x return in less than a year for Micron, its current valuation at 13x forward earnings and a low PEG ratio suggests further upside potential, supported by strong HBM margins and multi-year contracts.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Upgrades Cintas (CTAS) and Names it a Top Pick for 2026
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has upgraded Cintas (CTAS) to Overweight and raised its price target to $245 from $205, also naming it a top pick for 2026 due to solid fundamentals and pricing power. Separately, Cintas has submitted a renewed $3.96 billion takeover proposal for UniFirst, offering $275 per share in cash, aimed at consolidating the uniform rental market. This marks another attempt at acquisition, with Cintas expressing confidence in regulatory approvals.

feed.title: ResMed Q2 FY26 presentation: Revenue up 9%, expanding margins amid digital health push
feed.summary: ResMed (NYSE:RMD) reported strong Q2 FY26 results with a 9% revenue increase at constant currency and expanding margins. The company highlighted its leadership in connected and digital health, leveraging significant data assets and strategic initiatives, including a positive correlation between GLP-1 medications and PAP therapy adoption. ResMed projects high single-digit revenue growth and outpaced earnings growth over the next five years, driven by large underpenetrated markets and a robust innovation pipeline.

feed.title: Dare Bioscience launches preferred stock and warrant offering on Nasdaq
feed.summary: Dare Bioscience (NASDAQ:DARE) announced the initial closing of a Regulation A offering, issuing Series A Convertible Preferred Stock and warrants. The offering is part of a plan to raise capital, with each unit priced at $5.00, comprising one preferred share and two warrants. This financing comes as the microcap biotech is burning cash, though analysts have set price targets between $8 and $12 per share, and InvestingPro assesses its financial health as "FAIR."

feed.title: Stifel Revises Parker-Hannifin (PH) Outlook in Diversified Industrials Review
feed.summary: Stifel has raised its price target for Parker-Hannifin Corporation (NYSE:PH) to $941 from $869, while maintaining a Hold rating. This adjustment comes after Parker-Hannifin's strong performance over the past year, driven by its aerospace segment's growth, margin expansion, and successful acquisitions like Meggitt PLC. The company's "Win 3.0" framework has been effective in integrating new acquisitions, reinforcing investor confidence despite a challenging macroeconomic environment.

feed.title: Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) Reports Strong Q1 2026 Earnings, Beating Revenue and EPS Estimates
feed.summary: Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) significantly surpassed Wall Street expectations for its fiscal Q1 2026, driven by record iPhone and Services revenue. The company reported revenue of $143.8 billion and diluted EPS of $2.84, beating estimates. Shares rose slightly in after-hours trading, reflecting investor confidence despite recent tech sector volatility.

feed.title: US judge tosses lawsuit accusing J&J of fraud over talc bankruptcy strategy
feed.summary: A U.S. district judge has dismissed a lawsuit alleging Johnson & Johnson committed fraud by using a shell company's bankruptcy to resolve tens of thousands of talc-related lawsuits. The judge ruled that the cancer victims could not prove they were harmed by the bankruptcy delays, stating their claimed injury was hypothetical and incompatible with bankruptcy law. J&J's worldwide vice president of litigation welcomed the decision.

feed.title: Gilead Sciences CEO O’Day sells $1.39 million in stock
feed.summary: Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD) CEO Daniel Patrick O’Day sold nearly $1.39 million worth of company stock through two transactions on January 28, 2026. The sales were part of a pre-arranged trading plan, occurred with the stock near its 52-week high, and followed recent positive analyst updates and promising clinical trial results for the company.

feed.title: Western Digital Reports Fiscal Second Quarter 2026 Financial Results
feed.summary: Western Digital Corporation announced its fiscal second quarter 2026 financial results, highlighting strong revenue growth and gross margin expansion due to disciplined execution and demand in the AI-driven data economy. The company also reported strong free cash flow generation and returned over 100% of it to shareholders. Looking ahead to the third quarter of 2026, Western Digital expects continued strong revenue growth and improved profitability, driven by data center demand and the adoption of high-capacity drives, and has declared a cash dividend of $0.125 per share.

feed.title: Deckers Outdoor Corp (NYSE:DECK) Surges on Strong Q3 Earnings and Raised Guidance
feed.summary: Deckers Outdoor Corp (NYSE:DECK) reported strong Q3 2026 earnings, surpassing analyst expectations with net sales of $1.96 billion and diluted EPS of $3.33, driven by significant growth in its HOKA brand. The company also raised its full-year guidance for net sales to $5.40-$5.43 billion and diluted EPS to $6.80-$6.85, along with aggressive share repurchases. Investors reacted positively, with shares surging over 13% after the announcement.

feed.title: Stryker Corp (NYSE:SYK) Beats Q4 EPS Estimates, Provides 2026 Guidance
feed.summary: Stryker Corp (NYSE:SYK) reported strong Q4 and full-year 2025 financial results, with non-GAAP EPS beating analyst estimates, although revenue slightly missed. The company showcased significant organic sales growth and expanded operating margins. Stryker also provided optimistic 2026 guidance, projecting organic net sales growth of 8.0% to 9.5% and adjusted EPS between $14.90 and $15.10.

feed.title: Microbial contamination prompts Neogen recall of pet wound solution
feed.summary: Neogen Corporation has issued a voluntary nationwide recall of all lots within expiry of its Neogen®Vet HYCOAT® Hyaluronate Sodium Sterile Solution for dogs, cats, and horses. The recall stems from microbial contamination found in certain lots of the 10 mL/50 mg product vials, which has led to adverse event reports in horses when used intraarticularly, inconsistent with its labeled use. While no adverse events have been reported for labeled topical use, the company is also recalling 2 mL/20 mg vials out of caution and is working with the FDA.
